page: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 32 vCPUs, corresponding to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type does not include local NVMe storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the m7i-flex.8xlarge demonstrates strong single-core performance, scoring 2023.0 in Geekbench single-core tests and achieving a top-tier result of 332.68 tokens per second in LLM text generation using a small 135M model. It also shows strong throughput in static web serving at 48.18 GB/sec and single-threaded gzip compression at 32.42 GB/sec.

As part of the m7i-flex family, this instance offers a cost-efficient alternative to standard m7i instances by utilizing a resource-density profile optimized for workloads that do not constantly require maximum CPU utilization, though it maintains dedicated CPU allocation. While it performs well in general-purpose tasks, it exhibits weaker performance in large-scale LLM text generation, such as 2.67 tokens per second with a 70B model, due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. It is highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, text and image processing, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference using its built-in AMX instructions.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix math acceleration. It does not feature local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in single-core tasks, static web serving, and small-model LLM text generation, though it exhibits weaker capabilities for large-scale LLM inference due to the absence of a GPU. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, web serving, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 6 Gbps network baseline, optimized for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i-flex.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for web serving, database operations, and text processing.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8488C at 3.2 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated computing
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Strong performance in static web serving and single-core tasks
Suited for general-purpose applications, databases, and text processing
